reason: Implementation is a pure in-memory keyword matcher; none of the SQL-specific acceptance criteria are met. (1) No SQL ILIKE on the question column — uses Python `kw in text` substring check instead. (2) No `is_active = TRUE` filter — rules have no such field. (3) Confidence scoring is `matched/total_keywords` (yields 1.0 for a single-keyword exact hit and 0.5 for 1-of-2 partial hit); the SRS mandates fixed values of 0.95 for exact and 0.7 for partial. (4) No version field on rules, no `ORDER BY version DESC` / take-max logic. (5) No-match path returns `QueryResult(confidence=0, source='escalate')` instead of `KnowledgeResult(id=-1, source='escalate')` — wrong type entirely. The `KnowledgeResult` import from `v2` exists but is never used in the public API. Tests exercise the in-memory implementation's behaviour only and do not verify any SQL, is_active, versioning, or correct fixed-confidence semantics, so green tests here do not validate the SRS requirements.
gaps: SQL ILIKE on question column not implemented — in-memory substring used instead
ANY(keywords) SQL operator absent — Python loop used instead
is_active = TRUE filter missing from rules schema and query logic
Exact-match confidence must be 0.95, partial must be 0.7 — current formula is matched/len(keywords)
No version field; no ORDER BY version DESC / take-highest-version logic
No-match return type must be KnowledgeResult(id=-1, source='escalate'), not QueryResult
Tests do not cover SQL, is_active, versioning, or fixed-confidence thresholds
